elements.sent-2411-001: A subset $M$ of vertices in a graph $G$ is a mutual-visibility set if for any two vertices $u,v\in{M}$ there exists a shortest $u$-$v$ path in $G$ that contains no elements of $M$ as internal vertices.
elements.sent-2411-002: Let $\chi_{\mu (G)$ be the least number of colors needed to color the vertices of $G$, so that each color class is a mutual-visibility set.}
elements.sent-2411-004: It has been shown that the maximum size of a mutual-visibility set in $Q_{n}$ is at least $\Omega(2^{n )$.}
elements.sent-2411-005: Klav\v{z ar, Kuziak, Valenzuela-Tripodoro, and Yero further asked whether it is true that $\chi_{\mu (Q_{n )=O(1)$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-006: In this note we answer their question in the negative by showing that \begin{equation}\omega(1)=\chi_{\mu}(Q_{n})=O(\log\log{n}).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-008: A subset $M\subseteq{V(G)}$ of vertices is called a mutual-visibility set if any two vertices $u,v\in{M}$ can ``see'' each other, that is, there exists a shortest $u$-$v$ path in $G$ that contains no elements of $M$ as internal vertices.
elements.sent-2411-009: As in all other extremal problems, we are interested in the largest size of a mutual-visibility set in a given graph $G$, denoted as $\mu(G)$.
elements.sent-2411-010: The systematic investigation was pioneered by Di Stefano\cite{di2022mutual and has garnered extensive attention and subsequent research\cite[etc.]{axenovich2024visibility,boruzanli2024mutual,brevsar2024lower,cicerone2024cubelike,cicerone2023variety,cicerone2023mutual,cicerone2024mutual,cicerone2024diameter,korvze2024mutual,korvze2024variety in recent years.}}
elements.sent-2411-011: As the mutual-visibility problem was initially motivated by establishing efficient and confidential communication in networks, the research on $\mu(G)$ mainly focuses on sparse and highly connected graphs, such as product graphs and hypercube-like graphs.
elements.sent-2411-012: For $n\in\mathbb{N}$, the $n$-dimensional hypercube $Q_{n}$ is a graph on the vertex set $2^{[n]}$, such that two vertices $A,B\in2^{[n]}$ form an edge in $Q_{n}$ if and only if their symmetric difference $A\Delta{B :=(A\backslash{B )\cup(B\backslash{A )$ has size $1$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: It is known that $Q_{n}$ contains a large mutual-visibility set.
elements.sent-2411-014: Recently, Klav\v{z ar, Kuziak, Valenzuela-Tripodoro, and Yero\cite{klavzar2024color introduced the coloring version of the mutual-visibility problem.}}
elements.sent-2411-015: Given a coloring of the vertices of $G$, we say that $G$ is properly colored if each color class is a mutual-visibility set in $G$.
elements.sent-2411-016: The function considered in their paper is $\chi_{\mu (G)$, which is the least number of colors needed to properly color $V(G)$.}
elements.sent-2411-017: Equivalently, $\chi_{\mu (G)$ is the smallest integer such that $V(G)$ can be partitioned into $\chi_{\mu (G)$ mutual-visibility sets.}}
elements.sent-2411-018: It is easy to see that $\chi_{\mu (G)\geq\lvert{V(G)}\rvert/\mu(G)$.}
elements.sent-2411-019: Naturally, one might ask whether $\chi_{\mu (G)=O\left(\lvert{V(G)}\rvert/\mu(G)\right)$ could be true in general.}
elements.sent-2411-020: Given that $\mu(Q_{n )=\Omega(Q_{n )$, Klav\v{z ar, Kuziak, Valenzuela-Tripodoro, and Yero\cite{klavzar2024color raised the following question: \begin{center Is there an absolute constant $C>0$, such that $\chi_{\mu(Q_{n )\leq{C}$ holds for all $n\in\mathbb{N}$? \end{center We answer their question in the negative with the following result.}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-021: In particular, this shows that $\chi_{\mu (G)$ can be arbitrarily far from the trivial lower bound $\lvert{V(G)}\rvert/\mu(G)$.}
